Title: Genome-enabled discovery of anthraquinone biosynthesis in Senna tora

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase-like (CHS-L) family
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive compounds associated with antimicrobial and antiparasitic activities and potential prevention or delay of neurodegenerative diseases and diabetes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High expression in developing seeds at stage 4.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High expression in developing seeds at stage 4.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate prioritization by expression profiling during seed development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate positive contributor to anthraquinone biosynthesis, but not biochemically validated in this study.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS-L6 was one of two CHS-L genes highly induced at seed stage 4 when anthraquinones start to accumulate. 2. It shares high amino acid sequence similarity with CHS-L9. 3. The paper treats it as a candidate, but no direct enzyme assay validation was reported.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary engineering target after functional validation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not proven to catalyze anthraquinone scaffold formation.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase-like (CHS-L) family
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive compounds associated with antimicrobial and antiparasitic activities and potential prevention or delay of neurodegenerative diseases and diabetes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed at stage 6 of seed development.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed at stage 6 of seed development.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling during seed development; sequence similarity comparison
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate CHS-L family member potentially associated with anthraquinone biosynthesis, without direct functional proof.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STO02G027180 is a CHS-L family gene highly expressed at stage 6 of seed development. 2. It is sequence-similar to CHS-L9 and CHS-L6. 3. No direct biochemical evidence was provided for anthraquinone scaffold synthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential later-stage candidate for pathway engineering pending validation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate only; not experimentally validated as anthraquinone enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase-like (CHS-L) family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes successive condensations of malonyl-CoA to form anthranoid scaffold intermediates, producing atrochrysone carboxylic acid and endocrocin anthrone.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive compounds associated with antimicrobial and antiparasitic activities and potential prevention or delay of neurodegenerative diseases and diabetes.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: mature seeds; also other plant parts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds, especially stage 4 when anthraquinone accumulation begins.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds, especially stage 4.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the first committed step of plant anthraquinone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-guided candidate selection; seed transcriptomics; metabolite profiling; heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay with malonyl-CoA and 13C3-malonyl-CoA; HRMS and MS/MS product identification; heat-denatured enzyme control; CHS control comparison
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of anthranoid scaffold intermediates for anthraquinone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS-L9 was identified from a lineage-specifically expanded CHS-L family in Senna tora. 2. Its expression peaks at seed stage 4, coinciding with onset of anthraquinone accumulation. 3. Purified CHS-L9 converted malonyl-CoA into atrochrysone carboxylic acid and endocrocin anthrone. 4. The study concludes CHS-L9 carries out the first committed step of anthraquinone biosynthesis via the polyketide pathway in plants.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, RNA-seq, metabolomics, Hi-C, GBS; GenBank BioProject PRJNA605066; genome assembly accession JAAIUW000000000
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for breeding or engineering high-anthraquinone medicinal Senna cultivars; can be combined with downstream tailoring enzymes once identified to enhance bioactive anthraquinone content.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential metabolic flux diversion from other polyketide pathways not established.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Final oxidized anthraquinones such as emodin were not produced in vitro, implying additional downstream enzymes are required; other CHS-L genes may also participate.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to generate naringenin chalcone, with bisnoryangonin and p-coumaroyltriacetic acid lactone as shunt products.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: naringenin chalcone; bisnoryangonin; p-coumaroyltriacetic acid lactone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay with p-coumaroyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA; product analysis by UPLC-PDA and HRMS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in flavonoid biosynthesis and does not produce anthraquinone intermediates under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STO03G058250 was selected as a CHS-family control distinct from CHS-L9. 2. It did not produce anthraquinone intermediates from malonyl-CoA alone. 3. With p-coumaroyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA it generated naringenin chalcone and shunt products, confirming canonical CHS activity in flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily useful as a comparator distinguishing anthraquinone-forming CHS-L activity from canonical flavonoid CHS activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as biochemical control rather than the main anthraquinone gene.
